completed_steps.summary: Classified repository as a monolith with 1 part: a Python backend/kernel project with CLI, library, SQLite, MCP, platform, package, and game-engine traits.
Discovered canonical docs, archived stubs, pre-BMAD archives, BMAD round reports, research reports, CI config, and pyproject metadata; user provided no extra focus beyond the default rescan scope.

Tech stack identified as Python 3.11+ package aigm-kernel with PyYAML/jsonschema runtime deps, optional MCP server support, pytest/ruff/coverage/build/twine dev tooling, GitHub Actions CI on Python 3.11 and 3.12, SQLite migrations/schemas, and layered kernel-service architecture.

Exhaustive conditional analysis completed for public surfaces, SQLite migrations, JSON schemas, examples, tests, docs, MCP profiles, SaveManager pending confirmation, platform prewarm/sidecar, and BMAD archive/canonical document status; wrote api-contracts.md and data-models.md.

Generated annotated source tree analysis reflecting current monolith layout, rpg_engine package ownership, canonical docs, Round 4C stubs/archive, BMAD output tree, packaged resources, tests, and runtime-data boundaries.

Extracted development, CI, quality gate, documentation-only, and public repository boundary information from README, pyproject, CI workflow, canonical development/testing docs, and CLI help; updated development-guide.md.

Generated architecture.md for the single engine part from canonical architecture, code surface scan, packaged resource boundaries, player-safe flow, MCP/platform profiles, AI intent trust boundaries, and BMAD provenance.
Generated supporting documents for project overview, component inventory, and documentation provenance audit, refreshing Round 1 scan materials into strict GDS full_rescan/exhaustive outputs.
Generated master index linking all current scan outputs, canonical docs, old-document usage rules, key findings, and validation/review next steps.
Validation and user review complete; user selected done at the review checkpoint.
Workflow complete; final summary report created and state file finalized.

project_classification.architecture_type: local-first AI GM engine kernel with CLI, MCP adapter, runtime facade, SQLite state, package services, validation, projection, and AI intent orchestration

project_types.display_name: Python backend/kernel with CLI and library traits

existing_documentation.summary: Current docs live under docs/ with docs/index.md as canonical entry; legacy docs/specs/guides/architecture paths are Round 4C archived stubs; historical source text lives under docs/archive; BMAD scan and migration artifacts live under _bmad-output; time-boxed probes live under reports.

technology_stack.justification: pyproject.toml requires Python >=3.11 and CI runs 3.11/3.12.
pyproject.toml defines project metadata and console scripts aigm/rpg_engine.
Campaign/package YAML and JSON schema validation are first-class runtime paths.
Optional mcp extra and rpg_engine.mcp_adapter expose profile-gated tools.
rpg_engine.db owns SQLite connection, seed, migrations, and save schema constants.
pyproject optional dev deps and CI execute pytest, ruff, coverage, build, and twine check.

architecture_patterns.pattern: layered local-first kernel with thin adapters
architecture_patterns.summary: CLI, MCP, and platform surfaces delegate to kernel services such as SaveManager, GMRuntime, validation, commit, projection, context, and package services; SQLite and JSON/YAML resources provide local persistence and package contracts.

batches_completed.summary: Scanned CLI, runtime, save manager, MCP adapter, platform, AI intent, context, actions, packages, resources, validation, projection, and maintenance modules; public surfaces delegate to kernel services.

Scanned test inventory covering current native packages, AI intent, MCP/profile transcript boundaries, platform prewarm/sidecar, SaveManager, projection, validation, CLI, package services, and maintenance tooling.
Scanned packaged migrations through 0008, JSON schemas, packaged examples, root mirrors, and campaign fixtures; packaged resources are ahead of root migration mirror for intent preflight hardening.

Scanned canonical docs, Round 4C stubs/archive, BMAD round reports, research probes, and CI; canonical docs already govern archive precedence, but earlier BMAD-style reports lacked strict skill provenance.

conditional_analysis.api_contracts: No HTTP API found; public surface contracts are CLI, MCP tools, player/save/campaign/play/platform commands, and GMRuntime/SaveManager entrypoints. Output written to api-contracts.md.
conditional_analysis.data_models: SQLite migrations, JSON schemas, campaign package examples, Save Package state, preflight cache, projection state, and runtime workspace files scanned. Output written to data-models.md.
conditional_analysis.state_management: No frontend state store; state is SQLite plus workspace JSON under .aigm and derived projection artifacts.
conditional_analysis.deployment_config: GitHub Actions CI found; no container/Kubernetes deployment config found.

source_tree.summary: Single Python package with CLI/MCP/platform adapters, kernel services, packaged resources, canonical docs, archived legacy docs, test suite, and local runtime-data directories that must not become source truth.

development_operations.summary: Python 3.11+ editable install with dev,mcp extras; local quality gates use pytest, ruff, coverage, build, twine, markdown link checks, and JSON state validation; GitHub Actions runs Python 3.11/3.12 matrix plus installed CLI smoke and package build.
development_operations.deployment: No Docker/Kubernetes/Terraform/deployment config found; CI is the operational automation surface.
development_operations.contribution: No standalone CONTRIBUTING file found; contribution/development guidance lives in docs/development-guide.md, AGENTS.md, and BMAD governance docs.

architecture_documentation.summary: Architecture output now reflects the current local-first layered kernel: thin CLI/MCP/platform adapters, SaveManager player-safe gate, GMRuntime runtime facade, AI intent as advisory candidate chain, proposal/validation/commit write boundary, SQLite package state, and canonical docs/archive precedence.
architecture_documentation.skipped_multi_part_step: Step 7 integration architecture skipped because project classification is single-part monolith.

supporting_documentation.summary: Supporting docs now cover current project identity, V1 boundaries, component responsibilities, test coverage map, document trust layering, strict BMAD provenance, old document handling, packaging gaps, and resource mirror drift.

index_navigation.summary: index.md now links all generated outputs, canonical docs, archive usage rules, key findings, and the validation/review checkpoint.

resume_instructions: Workflow complete. Use _bmad-output/index.md as the entry point for brownfield PRD or later BMAD/GDS workflows.

Packaged migrations/schemas are ahead of root mirrors; use packaged resources as authority until mirrors are reconciled.
